description_long: This house features a rustic, wooden gabled roof with visible wooden beams and a prominent central dormer. The exterior walls are clad in grayish stone or shingle-like siding, giving it a sturdy, traditional appearance. A covered front porch with wooden railings and steps leads up to the main entrance, which is slightly recessed and flanked by decorative elements. The side of the house reveals a large, weathered water wheel, suggesting a historical or rural influence, possibly a converted mill house. Windows are few and mostly rectangular, with some boarded up, adding to the aged, utilitarian feel. The overall structure is compact, with two visible stories and a simple, functional layout.
description: A rustic two-story house with a wooden gabled roof, stone siding, a covered porch, and a large water wheel on the side.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden gabled roof, central dormer, covered porch with railings, and main entrance steps.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the large water wheel attached to the house and the end of the porch.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain roof and back wall with minimal features and no visible entryway.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a small set of steps, boarded window, and additional decorative wheels.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with some boarded windows
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with wooden railings, central steps, and a recessed main door
